title: AP Physics C: E&M 2018年国际卷

scoring.note: Scoring unavailable until answer keys are imported.

sections.title: Section II - Free Response
sections.directions: You have the indicated time to answer the following free response questions. It is not enough to answer a question by merely listing facts. You should present a cogent argument based on your critical analysis.

questions.prompt: If the magnitude of the electric field at point X is E, what is the magnitude of the electric field at point Y? (A) 4 (B) 2 (C) E (D) 2 E (E) 4 E
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: A positive point charge positive q is released from rest at point X. If the magnitude of the electric field at point X is E, what is the kinetic energy of the charge at point Y? (A) q E d (B) the square root of 2, end root, times q E d (C) 2 q E d (D) 2 times the square root of 2, end root, times q E d (E) 4 q E d Unauthorized copying or reuse of any part of this page is illegal. -7- The figure presents a part of a circuit containing two capacitors, C sub 1 and C sub 2, connected in parallel. Two points, A and B, are indicated on the circuit. Capacitor C sub 1 is on the left vertical branch and capacitor C sub 2 is on the right vertical branch. Point A is indicated on the top horizontal branch, and point B is indicated on the bottom horizontal branch. At these points, vertical wires are shown coming from the circuit. Point A is directly above point B.

questions.prompt: <span aria-label="A coaxial cable is composed of a central conducting wire surrounded by a cylindrical insulator, and around that is a conducting cylindrical shell with an insulating cover. A power supply of e m fepsilon is used at one end to connect the two conducting regions, and a resistive load of resistance R is connected at the other end of the cable to complete the circuit, as shown in the schematic diagram and circuit figure above. Each of the following two figures present a cross section of the preceding coaxial cable from the perspective of the battery end. The central conducting wire is shown as a circle, enclosed in a larger circle, which is the cylindrical insulator. In each figure, a point P inside the cylindrical insulator is up and to the right and a distance b from the center of the cable. This insulator is enclosed in a larger circle, which is the cylindrical conducting shell, which is also enclosed in the insulating cover. In figure 1, a point Q is indicated outside of the coaxial cable entirely to the left of the center of the cable. In figure 2, there are no additional points indicated. A cross section of the cable from the perspective of the power supply end is depicted above as if looking into the left end of the cable. Point P is inside the cylindrical insulator a distance b from the center of the cable. Clearly mark this loop as “Loop 2.” 3 On figure 2 shown above right, draw an arrow to show the direction of the magnetic field at point P. The arrow should start on and point away from the point in the direction of the magnetic field. Question 2 continues on the next page. Point P is 0.0050 meters from the center of the cable. The plot of the magnetic field strength B as a function of the e m f epsilon of the power supply is shown below.
